blocks.content: It was after one in the morning. The five of them were still in the living room.

Cade had his eyes closed, head back, not sleeping — just somewhere. Sophie had stopped crying and moved into a quieter kind of distress, the kind that doesn't have a sound. Wren sat with her hands in her lap and watched the deputies move in and out of the house. Dean had made coffee nobody touched.
Nadia had her phone out. She was looking at the screen.

blocks.speaker: Dean
blocks.content: They're not going to let us leave.

blocks.speaker: Sophie
blocks.content: I don't want to be in this house.

blocks.speaker: Dean
blocks.content: We don't have a choice right now.

blocks.speaker: Sophie
blocks.content: Don't say I know again. Say something else.
Dean opened his mouth. Closed it.

blocks.speaker: Cade
blocks.content: We're going to get through tonight. That's all we need to do right now. Get through tonight.
His eyes were still closed. But his voice was steady and it was the right thing to say and the room held it.
